statement.en: ESGE recommends the best practice snare placement, capture, and closure technique described below for cold snare EMR.

text.en: Use of a dedicated, thin-wire (0.3-mm wire diameter) cold snare is strongly recommended when performing piecemeal cold snare polypectomy.
A maximum snare diameter of 10 mm is recommended for piecemeal cold snare polypectomy as tissue transection of pieces > 10 mm regularly leads to stalling of snare transection.
Start at the edge of the lesion.
Aim to capture a rim of 1–3 mm of normal mucosa (Fig. 2, images A4 and C1). This is critical in cold snare polypectomy owing to the absence of electrosurgical energy.
Ideally the long axis of the snare should be placed parallel to the polyp base.
The V of the snare (Fig. 2, images C1 and C2) should be extended just beyond the tip of the snare catheter prior to snare placement.

subItems.source_span: Hand-extracted from source PDF after careful cross-rec attribution; PDF column flow had scattered these sub-items across the narrative subsection containing R17.
